result:
## kernel/params.c ŌĆö kmalloc/kzalloc/kmalloc_array call sites

Found via ccodegraph (`sql` escape hatch, cross-checked with grep) ŌĆö 4 sites total, no `kmalloc_array` in this file.

**1. `kernel/params.c:51`** ŌĆö `kmalloc(sizeof(*p) + size, GFP_KERNEL)` in `kmalloc_parameter()` (line 47)
- **(b) Ownership transferred.** On success, `p` is linked into the global `kmalloced_params` list (line 56) and `p->val` is returned to the caller. The only caller is `param_set_charp()` (line 277), which stores the pointer into `*kp->arg` ŌĆö the kernel_param's own storage. It is later freed by `maybe_kfree_parameter()` (line 63ŌĆō76, `kfree(p)` at **line 71**), invoked either from a subsequent `param_set_charp()` call (line 272) or from module/param teardown elsewhere. NULL-checked (line 52) ŌĆö no leak on the allocation-failure path since nothing is allocated.

**2. `kernel/params.c:639`** ŌĆö `kzalloc(sizeof(*mk->mp), GFP_KERNEL)` in `add_sysfs_param()` (line 626)
- **(b) Ownership transferred**, not a leak. Assigned directly to `mk->mp` (the module_kobject passed in by the caller) at the point of allocation. On the immediately-following OOM check (line 640-641) the function just `return -ENOMEM` **without freeing** ŌĆö but the function's own doc comment (line 623-624) states "Always cleans up if there's an error" at the *caller* level, and the code at line 644-648 explicitly comments "Caller will cleanup via `free_module_param_attrs`". Confirmed: callers `mod_sysfs_setup` (line 717-719) and `mod_sysfs_setup`'s param-group step (line 729-731) call `free_module_param_attrs(&mod->mkobj)` (which does `kfree(mk->mp->grp.attrs)` / `kfree(mk->mp)` at lines 693-694) whenever `add_sysfs_param()` returns an error.

**3. `kernel/params.c:644`** ŌĆö `kzalloc(sizeof(mk->mp->grp.attrs[0]), GFP_KERNEL)` in `add_sysfs_param()` (line 626)
- **(b) Ownership transferred**, same story as #2: assigned to `mk->mp->grp.attrs`; on OOM (line 647-648) returns `-ENOMEM` without a local free, per the explicit comment "Caller will cleanup via free_module_param_attrs" ŌĆö cleaned up by the caller's `free_module_param_attrs()` (lines 693-694) on the error path.

**4. `kernel/params.c:772`** ŌĆö `kzalloc(sizeof(struct module_kobject), GFP_KERNEL)` in `locate_module_kobject()` (line 762)
- Guarded by `BUG_ON(!mk)` (line 773) ŌĆö allocation failure is not a normal error path (panics instead), so no free-on-NULL case applies.
- Success path: **(b) ownership transferred** ŌĆö `mk` is returned to the caller `kernel_add_sysfs_param()` (line 797) and becomes the module's kobject.
- Error path (`kobject_init_and_add`/`sysfs_create_file` fails, line 783-788): **not a direct `kfree`** ŌĆö the code calls `kobject_put(&mk->kobj)` (line 784), transferring cleanup responsibility to the kobject subsystem's refcounting/release mechanism, then returns `NULL`. Worth flagging: this file's own release callback for `module_ktype`, `module_kobj_release()` (line 945-949), does **not** call `kfree(mk)` ŌĆö it only does `complete(mk->kobj_completion)`. Since `kobj_completion` is zero-initialized by `kzalloc` and never set on this path, this error path looks suspicious (potential `complete(NULL)` and/or an actual leak of `mk`), but that's a kobject-lifecycle question outside plain kmalloc/kfree pairing in this file, not a straightforward "freed here" case ŌĆö flagging it rather than asserting a verdict.

### Summary table

| Line | Function | Fate |
|---|---|---|
| 51 | `kmalloc_parameter` | (b) transferred to `*kp->arg`; freed later via `maybe_kfree_parameter()` ŌåÆ `kfree` at line 71 |
| 639 | `add_sysfs_param` | (b) transferred to `mk->mp`; on error, freed by caller via `free_module_param_attrs()` (lines 693-694) |
| 644 | `add_sysfs_param` | (b) transferred to `mk->mp->grp.attrs`; on error, freed by caller via `free_module_param_attrs()` (lines 693-694) |
| 772 | `locate_module_kobject` | (b) success: transferred to caller as return value. error: handed to `kobject_put()` (line 784) ŌĆö release callback in this file doesn't `kfree`; flagged as needing kobject-lifecycle context outside this file, not a plain same-function free |

No allocation in this file is freed unconditionally in the same function (case c), and none are leaked outright ŌĆö all either transfer ownership or are eventually cleaned up by a paired helper (`maybe_kfree_parameter` / `free_module_param_attrs`), except the line-772 error path which merits closer kobject-refcounting review if you want certainty.
